packageRules.description: Group all GitHub Actions bumps into one PR.

The github-actions manager also extracts `node-version: 22` from actions/setup-node and wants to rewrite it to the exact current patch (22.23.1). That pin is deliberately floating - the runner should pick up 22.x security patches on its own - so suppress everything below a major. A move to Node 24 still surfaces on the Dependency Dashboard via the major rule at the bottom.

Cargo.toml declares loose requirements on purpose (`tauri = "2"`, `cpal = "0.15"`). The global `bump` strategy would rewrite each of those to an exact minor on every release, churning the manifest for no gain. `update-lockfile` instead moves Cargo.lock for in-range updates and only edits Cargo.toml when the new version falls outside the existing requirement. ci.yml runs `cargo check --locked`, so the refreshed lock is exactly what gets verified.

Cargo treats a bump of the leftmost non-zero component as breaking: cpal 0.15 -> 0.16 or windows-sys 0.60 -> 0.61 is a semver-MAJOR change. Renovate's cargo versioning is plain semver 2.0, so it labels these updateType `minor` and the major rule at the bottom never sees them. Open the PR and let CI compile both targets, but never automerge: several of these crates are ABI-coupled to something outside their own version (windows-sys must stay on the version Tauri already resolves, or the binary carries two copies; gtk is ignored outright for the same reason).

Tauri's Rust crates and its JS packages are released in lockstep, and a mismatch between them surfaces as an IPC/type error at runtime rather than a compile error - so they must land in a single PR. This is the one grouping Dependabot structurally cannot express, since its groups never span two ecosystems. Not automerged: ci.yml only runs `cargo check` + `vite build` and never launches the app, so a regression in the tray-icon / global-shortcut / autostart plugins would pass green.

The text-injection stack: clipboard (arboard), synthetic typing (enigo), XDG RemoteDesktop portal (ashpd). These sit on the paths the clipboard-restore and modifier-release fixes hardened, and ci.yml never injects a keystroke - so even a patch release here can change behaviour invisibly to a green build. Always gets a human.

atspi re-exports atspi-connection's types; the two versions must be compatible or the a11y-bus listener fails to build. Their feature flags are hand-tuned at the declaration site (p2p is deliberately off) - re-read that comment before merging.
wayland-protocols-misc is built against a specific wayland-client; bump them together.
rodio plays through cpal's backend and pins a cpal version; a lone bump of either duplicates the audio backend.
react and react-dom must ship the identical version, and their @types track the same major.
@tailwindcss/vite is versioned in lockstep with the tailwindcss v4 core.

@vitejs/plugin-react major-peer-depends on a matching vite major (v6 requires vite ^8 and imports vite's `./internal` subpath, which vite 7 does not export). A lone bump of either strands the other on an incompatible peer, so neither PR can ever go green alone -- they must bump as a pair.
The three variable fonts release together - one PR instead of three.
Major bumps require manual review - surface as checkboxes on the Dependency Dashboard, do not open the PR automatically, do not automerge. Minor/patch still flow through automerge gated by ci.
